items.title: India and Japan to Deepen Defence Ties
items.whyInNews: India and Japan have decided to deepen their defence ties, with a focus on cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, as announced on April 24, 2026, by Indian and Japanese officials.
items.summary: The decision to deepen defence ties between India and Japan was made with the aim of promoting peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region. This move is seen as a strategic response to China's growing military presence in the region. The two countries will enhance their cooperation in areas such as maritime security, counter-terrorism, and defence technology.
keyPoints: India and Japan to enhance cooperation in maritime security
Counter-terrorism measures to be strengthened between the two nations
Defence technology cooperation to be increased
Aim to promote peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region
importantPoints: Full form of ASEAN is Association of Southeast Asian Nations, founded in 1967, headquartered in Jakarta
Related constitutional article is Article 51 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with international relations
Historical context: This is the first time India and Japan have decided to deepen their defence ties in response to China's growing military presence
Key statistic: India and Japan are among the top 5 countries in the Asia-Pacific region in terms of military spending
Policy implication: This move is expected to strengthen India's position in the Indo-Pacific region and promote its national security interests
items.examRelevance: GK: International Relations, specifically India's relations with Japan and China

items.title: India to Host Fourth India-Africa Forum Summit
items.whyInNews: India will host the fourth India-Africa Forum Summit next month, as announced by the Indian government on April 24, 2026.
items.summary: The fourth India-Africa Forum Summit will be held in New Delhi, with the aim of strengthening India's relations with African countries. The summit will focus on areas such as trade, investment, and cooperation in areas like healthcare, education, and infrastructure development. This move is seen as a strategic response to China's growing presence in Africa.

Aim to strengthen India's relations with African countries
Focus on areas such as trade, investment, and cooperation in healthcare and education

Related constitutional article is Article 51 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with international relations
Historical context: This is the fourth India-Africa Forum Summit, with the first one being held in 2008
Key statistic: India's trade with Africa has grown by 20% in the last 5 years
Policy implication: This move is expected to strengthen India's position in Africa and promote its national interests

items.title: National Panchayati Raj Day to be Celebrated on April 24
items.whyInNews: The National Panchayati Raj Day will be celebrated on April 24, 2026, as announced by the Indian government.
items.summary: The National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated every year on April 24, to mark the anniversary of the 73rd Amendment to the Indian Constitution, which came into effect in 1993. The amendment gave constitutional status to the three-tier panchayati raj system, with the aim of promoting decentralization and grassroots democracy.
keyPoints: National Panchayati Raj Day to be celebrated on April 24
73rd Amendment to the Indian Constitution gave constitutional status to panchayati raj system
Aim to promote decentralization and grassroots democracy
Expected to strengthen local self-government in India
importantPoints: Full form of Panchayati Raj is system of local self-government, founded in 1993, headquartered in New Delhi
Related constitutional article is Article 40 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with organization of village panchayats
Historical context: The 73rd Amendment was a significant step towards promoting decentralization and grassroots democracy in India
Key statistic: There are over 250,000 panchayats in India, covering over 600,000 villages
Policy implication: This move is expected to strengthen local self-government in India and promote national development
items.examRelevance: Polity: Local Self-Government, specifically Panchayati Raj system

items.title: Rashtriya Safai Karigar Finance and Development Corporation Provides Concessional Finance
items.whyInNews: The Rashtriya Safai Karigar Finance and Development Corporation has provided concessional finance of Rs 223 crore in the financial year 2025-26.
items.summary: The Rashtriya Safai Karigar Finance and Development Corporation has provided concessional finance to support the development of safai karigars, who are engaged in cleaning and sanitation work. This move is seen as a significant step towards promoting the welfare of safai karigars and supporting their economic development.

items.title: NGT Notice to Six Cricket Stadiums Over Water Usage
items.whyInNews: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) issued a notice to six cricket stadiums over their water usage, as per a report on April 24, 2026. The green body had asked several cricket stadiums to provide information to the Central Ground Water Authority.
items.summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has issued a notice to six cricket stadiums regarding their water usage. The Central Ground Water Authority is tabulating the information disclosed by the stadiums. This move is aimed at conserving water and promoting sustainable practices in sports infrastructure. The NGT's notice is a significant step towards environmental conservation in India's sports sector.
keyPoints: Six cricket stadiums received notices from the NGT
The notice is related to the stadiums' water usage
The Central Ground Water Authority is involved in the process
The move aims to promote sustainable water usage in sports infrastructure
importantPoints: NGT - National Green Tribunal, founded in 2010, headquartered in New Delhi
Related to the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
First time the NGT has taken such action against cricket stadiums in India
India ranks 120th in the Water Quality Index, as per a report by the Central Pollution Control Board
This move is significant for India's environmental conservation efforts
items.examRelevance: GK: Environmental Conservation, Polity: Constitutional Bodies

items.title: IPL 2026: Samson, Akeal Heroes as CSK Decimates MI
items.whyInNews: Sanju Samson and Akeal Hosein were the heroes for Chennai Super Kings (CSK) as they decimated Mumbai Indians (MI) in an IPL 2026 match on April 24, 2026, at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai.
items.summary: Chennai Super Kings (CSK) defeated Mumbai Indians (MI) in an IPL 2026 match at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai. Sanju Samson scored his second hundred of the season, while Akeal Hosein took four wickets. The victory is CSK's biggest win by runs in the tournament's history. The match was played on April 24, 2026, and CSK's win has significant implications for the team's standings in the tournament.

India is ranked 2nd in the ICC ODI Team Rankings, as per the latest update
This win is significant for CSK's chances in the IPL 2026 tournament
